snow_or_rain_notes: Snow remains till Gaumukh and sometimes beyond until mid-May
Pre-winter snows at higher elevation campsites (Bhojbasa and above) sometime in October
Pre-monsoon rains add to river force
Monsoon downpours in July cause landslides and rockfalls on trail sections
temperature_notes: Summer day: 15°C to 20°C; night drops to almost 0°C
Autumn day: around 15°C; night at Tapovan can go 3-5°C below 0°C

stated_difficulty: Several difficult sections on this trek that require you to be alert and trek carefully
terrain_challenges: Large boulder crossing between Gangotri and Chirbasa with a drop into the river
Unstable 'Kachha Dhaang' sections between Chirbasa and Bhojbasa prone to landslides
Bhagirathi River crossing after Bhojbasa via government-provided trolleys/pulleys
Final ascent to Tapovan along lateral moraines of Gangotri glacier, rockfall-prone
Daily ascents and descents over ~41 km in six trekking days
altitude_or_weather_risks: Gangotri basecamp at 10,050 ft makes trekkers susceptible to Acute Mountain Sickness (AMS)
Trek goes over 14,000 ft at Tapovan; AMS risk is real above 10,000 ft
AMS can affect first-time and experienced trekkers alike; fitness does not eliminate risk if ascent is too fast
Rainfall or snowfall lasting hours can change trek dynamics; rain can flood trails or make them slushy
Snowfall can obstruct existing paths, requiring fresh tracks to be cut

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog or run 5 km in under 32 minutes
approval_requirements: Fitness approval from the Indiahikes team is required 20 days before the trek date
At least 3 runs from the last 1 month must meet the trek's fitness criteria
prep_window: 6 to 8 weeks before the trek begins
A 6-week plan is provided for those not currently active

experience_themes: Snow on trail and natural snow bridges over Bhagirathi in early summer
Receding snow and sparse vegetation turning green by mid-summer
Swelled Bhagirathi river from melting snow and pre-monsoon rains
Autumn colours (yellows, browns, maroons) on vegetation en route to Chirbasa
Deeper blue, gentler-flowing Bhagirathi in autumn
Clear skies and stable weather in autumn
primary_watchouts: Monsoon landslides and rockfalls on trail sections in July
Trail to Tapovan untenable in monsoons
Pre-winter snows at higher campsites (Bhojbasa and above) from October
Heavy pilgrim traffic on trail until Gaumukh in summer and continuing into July-August
Trekking in July/August due to monsoon landslides and rockfalls
best_for: Trekkers drawn to trekking in snow (summer)
Experiencing autumn colours and stable clear skies (autumn)
not_ideal_for: Trekking in July/August due to monsoon landslides and rockfalls
Trekking after mid-October due to pre-winter snows at higher campsites
Trekkers seeking solitude in summer (heavy pilgrim traffic)
